text:
Once upon a sunlit afternoon in a forest filled with gentle rustling leaves, a hare named Hazel had an extraordinary idea. She would organize an extravagant tea party just for hares. Hazel scoured the forest for china dishes, lemon cakes, and lavender-scented sachets. She spread out a beautiful rug of softest moss and decorated with dainty flowers.

Her friends were delighted. There was Flicker with her endless wingspan of lacewing flies; Bolden with his bold brown boots; Maudlin with her melodious owlet song; and every hare in the forest felt special.

On the day of the party, the sun shone brightly, and birds sang lighthearted tunes. Hares played leapfrog across the lawn.

Then, during a delicate moment, a wise old owl hovered above observing the fun. She hooted softly, feeling a bit melancholy.

“Why such cheerful gathering?” the owl asked.

“Oh,” replied Hazel with a gig
